description: city in Yolo County, California, United States
description_wiki: Davis is the most populous city in Yolo County, California, United States.

description: public university in the Davis, California area; part of the University of California system
description_wiki: The University of California, Davis (UC Davis, UCD, or Davis) is a public land-grant research university in the Davis, California area, United States.

description: island country in the southwest Pacific Ocean
description_wiki: New Zealand is an island country in the southwestern Pacific Ocean.

description: country in East Asia
description_wiki: Taiwan, officially the Republic of China (ROC), is a country in East Asia.

description: country in East Asia
description_wiki: China, officially the People's Republic of China (PRC), is a country in East Asia.

description: private research university in Stanford, California, USA
description_wiki: Leland Stanford Junior University, commonly referred to as Stanford University, is a private research university in Stanford, California, United States.

description: independent agency and volunteer program run by the United States Government
description_wiki: The Peace Corps is an independent agency and program of the United States government that trains and deploys volunteers to communities in partner countries around the world.

description: sovereign state in Africa
description_wiki: Somalia, officially the Federal Republic of Somalia, is the easternmost country in continental Africa.

description: capital and the largest city of Somalia
description_wiki: Mogadishu, locally known as Xamar or Hamar, is the capital and most populous city of Somalia.

description: year
description_wiki: 1968 (MCMLXVIII) was a leap year starting on Monday of the Gregorian calendar, the 1968th year of the Common Era (CE) and Anno Domini (AD) designations, the 968th  year of the 2nd millennium, the 68th  year of the 20th century, and the  9th   year of the 1960s decade.

description: Native American reservation in Dakota, United States
description_wiki: The Standing Rock Sioux Tribe of North & South Dakota controls the Standing Rock Reservation  (Lakota: Íŋyaŋ Woslál Háŋ), which straddles the border between North and South Dakota in the United States, and is inhabited by ethnic "Hunkpapa and Sihasapa bands of Lakota Oyate and the Ihunktuwona and Pabaksa bands of the Dakota Oyate," as well as the Hunkpatina Dakota (Lower Yanktonai).

description: primary river of the largest drainage basin in the United States
description_wiki: The Mississippi River is the primary river of the largest drainage basin in the United States.

description: U.S. manufacturer of agricultural machinery and construction equipment
description_wiki: The International Harvester Company (often abbreviated IH or International) was an American manufacturer of agricultural and construction equipment, automobiles, commercial trucks, lawn and garden products, household equipment, and more.

surface_forms: southern Somalia was colonized by the Italians
description: colony of the Kingdom of Italy in Somalia
description_wiki: Italian Somaliland (Italian: Somalia Italiana; Arabic: الصومال الإيطالي, romanized: Al-Sumal Al-Italiy; Somali: Dhulka Soomaalida ee Talyaaniga) comprised self-ruling protectorates and colonial possessions of the Kingdom of Italy in present-day Somalia.

aliases: the Union of Soviet Socialist Republics
description: former country in Eurasia (1922–1991)
description_wiki: The Union of Soviet Socialist Republics (USSR), also known as the Soviet Union, was a transcontinental country that spanned much of Eurasia from 1922 until its dissolution in 1991.

description: country located primarily in North America
description_wiki: The United States of America (USA), also known as the United States (U.S.) or America, is a country primarily located in North America.

description: private research university in Stanford, California, USA
description_wiki: Leland Stanford Junior University, commonly referred to as Stanford University, is a private research university in Stanford, California, United States.

description: student activist movement, defunct
description_wiki: Students for a Democratic Society (SDS) was a national student activist organization in the United States during the 1960s and was one of the principal representations of the New Left.

aliases: Black Pather Party For Self Defense
description: US Black power organization (1966–1982)
description_wiki: The Black Panther Party (BPP; originally the Black Panther Party for Self-Defense) was an American Marxist–Leninist and black power political and militant organization founded by college students Bobby Seale and Huey P. Newton in October 1966 in Oakland, California and active in that area until 1982.

description: law school of Yale University
description_wiki: Yale Law School (YLS) is the law school of Yale University, a private Ivy League research university in New Haven, Connecticut.

description: President of the United States from 1993 to 2001 (born 1946)
description_wiki: William Jefferson  Clinton (né Blythe III; born August 19, 1946) is an American politician and lawyer  who served as the 42nd president of the United States from 1993 to 2001.

description: American politician and diplomat (born 1947)
description_wiki: Hillary Diane Rodham Clinton (née Rodham; born October 26, 1947) is an American politician, lawyer, and diplomat.

description: city in Connecticut, United States
description_wiki: New Haven is a city in the U.S. state of Connecticut.

description: American association of lawyers
description_wiki: The National Lawyers Guild (NLG) is a progressive  public interest association of lawyers, law students, paralegals, jailhouse lawyers, law collective members, and other activist legal workers in the United States.

description: Romance language originating in the Iberian Peninsula
description_wiki: Spanish (español) or Castilian (castellano) is a Romance language of the Indo-European language family that evolved from the Vulgar Latin spoken on the Iberian Peninsula of Europe.

description: city in and county seat of King County, State of Washington, United States
description_wiki: Seattle (  see-AT-əl) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Washington and the Pacific Northwest region of North America.

description: city in and county seat of Snohomish County, Washington, United States of America
description_wiki: Everett ( EV-er-it; Lushootseed: dᶻəɬigʷəd [d͜zəˈɬi̞ɡʷəd]) is the county seat of and the most populous city in Snohomish County, Washington, United States.

description: city in Skagit County, Washington, United States
description_wiki: Mount Vernon is the county seat of and the most populous city in Skagit County, Washington, United States.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Texas (  TEK-səss) is the most populous state in the Southern United States.

surface_forms: southern tip of Texas near Brownsville
description: seat of Cameron County, Texas, United States
description_wiki: Brownsville ( BROWNZ-vil) is a city in the U.S. state of Texas and the largest city and county seat of Cameron County, located on the western Gulf Coast in South Texas, adjacent to the border with Matamoros, Tamaulipas, Mexico.

description: American labor organization
description_wiki: The United Farm Workers of America, or more commonly just United Farm Workers (UFW), is a labor union for farmworkers in the United States.

description: North American tribe
